summary: Defined validation layer approach, structured testing plan for cross-AI handoff, and improved scanning strategy.
goals: Build a system that allows seamless switching between AI platforms without losing context
Ensure exported handoff can reliably reconstruct project context in a new AI session
Introduce a validation layer to protect project memory integrity
Enhance project scanning to extract structured insights (tech stack, architecture, purpose)
rules: All AI updates must conform to schema_version before merging
Never overwrite core project fields without validation pass
Preserve original project memory as fallback before applying updates
decisions: Adopt manual-first approach for AI platform integration before exploring APIs
Use schema-based validation (JSON schema or TypeScript types) before merging AI updates
Store a backup snapshot before every AI merge operation
currentState: Core functionality is working. Next phase focuses on reliability (validation), accuracy (project scanning), and proving cross-platform continuity via testing.
nextSteps: Test whether a new AI chat can continue the project correctly from the exported handoff
Improve project scanning so uploaded folders produce stronger project understanding
Add validation to prevent bad AI updates from breaking project memory
Create a test: export handoff → paste into new AI chat → verify continuity
Design JSON schema validation for project memory structure
Implement 'safe merge' function with validation + rollback
Improve project scanner to detect frameworks, languages, and folder intent
Add scoring system for scan confidence (low/medium/high understanding)
openQuestions: Should Project Brain support direct API connections to AI platforms or remain manual-first?
What minimum data is required for a successful cross-AI handoff?
Should scan results be editable by the user before being committed to memory?
How strict should validation be before rejecting an AI update?

aiInstructions.focus: Help move the project forward without losing continuity
